text: As you change the dimensions of a rectangular prism by a certain scale factor, how do the surface area and volume of the prism change? How is creating similar rectangular prisms like creating similar rectangles? How is it different?

If you change the dimensions of a rectangular prism by a scale factor, f, the surface area will change by the scale factor squared, f2, and the volume will change by the scale factor cubed, f3.

Creating similar rectangular prisms is enlarging or shrinking each dimension by the same scale factor. It is similar way of creating similar rectangles, which is enlarging or shrinking each dimension by the same scale factor. You use proportional reasoning for both situations. The difference is that you have three dimensions (width, length, height) in rectangular prism, whereas there are two dimensions (length, width) in rectangles. In particular, when creating a box to hold a specific volume, while using the least material, is like looking for the least perimeter for a specified area.

Did You Know?

Earthworms play an important role in food production. Fonte and his colleagues Nathan Mueller and Marian Hsieh, supported by a U.S. National Science Foundation Graduate Research Fellowship studied the contributions of earthworms in food production by overlaying and analyzing maps of the population of earthworms around the world. Their findings showed that earthworms play an important role in grain production - for example, 10% of grain production in sub-Saharan Africa, and 8% in Latin America and the Caribbean. Fonte said this makes sense, "because farmers there tend to have less access to fertilizer and pesticides. Instead, they rely more on earthworm - rich organic matter like manure and crop residues, which help stimulate the beneficial effect earthworms have on plants."

In general earthworms contribute about 6.5% of the increase of grain production and 2.3% of the increase in legumes each year around the world. Their findings suggest that earthworms may account for as much " as 140 million metric tons of food produced annually - roughly comparative to the amount of cereal grains (rice, wheat, rye, oats, barely, maize and millet) grown annually by Russia, the world's fourth-largest producer."

Their study may be a first of its kind in that it sought to investigate one piece of soil diversity and its value on a global scale. Earthworms build healthy soil by decomposing organic matter that produce rich nutrients and soils structure that can hold water. This also decreases the dependency on other fertilizers or insect repellents.

Source: U.S. National Science Foundation, "Earthworms Contribute to 6.5% of Global Grain Production," November 9. 2023.
